results.title: Vietnam: The Ten Thousand Day War. - Volume 6.
results.creator_credit: Don North, Ian Macleod, Paul Lang, Ron Kelly, Cinequity Funding and Information Television Productions
results.brief_description: Peace - is a history of the search for peace throughout the war. The ultimate ceasefire is seen by the U.S. as ‘peace with honour’, and by Saigon as a sellout. “Surrender” - examines the final offensive in 1975, two years after America’s withdrawal. “The Unsung Soldiers” - views the silent homecoming of the veterans. The post war world is unrewarding and sometimes hostile towards men who fought in America’s first ‘defeat’.

results.title: Vietnam: The Ten Thousand Day War. - Volume 5.
results.creator_credit: Don North, Ian Macleod, Paul Lang, Ron Kelly, Cinequity Funding and Information Television Productions
results.brief_description: Soldiering On - is a study of the morale and battlefield conditions of the American soldier in Vietnam. It also covers the controversy surrounding the American prisoners-of-war. “The Village War” - examines the constant struggle for control of the 16,000 villages in South Vietnam, and the beginning of the ‘Vietnamisation’ process.

results.title: Winners [Series]
results.brief_description: ‘Winners’ and ‘More Winners’ are anthology series of fourteen telemovies, featuring historical drama, comedy, science fiction, fantasy, adventure and social realism. Each film celebrates individuality and the facing of difficult situations with courage and ingenuity.

results.title: The Tigger movie [DVD]
results.creator_credit: Jun Falkenstein, Cheryl Abood and Walt Disney Pictures
results.brief_description: When no-one will play with him Tigger sets out on a journey to find his family tree, and other Tiggers. This is harder than he thought and he quickly becomes discouraged. When his friends Winnie the Pooh, Eeyore, Owl, Kanga and Roo see that their friend is sad, they pretend to be the family he wishes for, by writing him a letter and dressing up as Tiggers. But when Tigger finds out who they really are, he becomes more determined to find his real family, and wanders out into a snowstorm. Roo, upset that he has lost his best friend, convinces the others to follow Tigger and show him that they are his real family. Based on classic characters created by A.A. Milne. This animated feature length film is filled with the songs, colour and movement that young children adore.

results.title: The Tide of traffic
results.creator_credit: Derek Williams, Humphrey Swingler, British Petroleum and Greenpark Productions
results.brief_description: Takes a world-wide look at our growing dependence on cars and road transport, and at the environmental impact of the congested motorways This film was BP’s contribution to the 1972 Stockholm United Nations Conference on the Human Environment.

results.title: Winners : The Other Facts of Life (Captioned)
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series ‘Winners’.
Ben can’t understand how people can live so happily with the injustice and disaster going on in the world. His family’s lack of response leads Ben to mount a one person awareness crusade. Nothing seems to distract Ben from the larger troubles of the world until a crisis causes him to focus his energies on loving and helping his family.

results.title: Winners : Tarflowers
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series ‘Winners’.
Garbage is magic, and so is Kev’s job. It’s an important job, and if he and Big Dog didn’t do it, who would? Mrs. Pearl catches stray people like the dog-catcher catches dogs, and every garbage night she and Bruiser hunt Kev through the streets of Newton.

results.title: Winners : Tarflowers (Captioned)
results.brief_description: Kev, and his dog, have a special job - to tend the Tarflowers. But his task is made difficult by Mrs. Pearl and her henchmen who catch stray people. However, Kev’s friend Mary, his mother and the garbage collectors know that the Tarflowers are really magic and that Kev isn’t as dumb as Mrs. Pearl thinks. Part of the “Winners” series.

results.title: Winners : Room to Move (Captioned)
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series ‘Winners’.
Carol is a dedicated runner, coached by her father, day and night. Then she makes friends with Angie, the new girl at school whose punk looks haven’t made her very popular. Angie introduces Carol to the wonderful world of dance. But Carol’s father won’t let her do anything that conflicts with her track-training. Parental pressure is tough on Carol who wants to succeed, but at several things. She rebels and plans a new life for herself. But the all-important race is coming up and Carol has to give it all she’s got. Part of the “Winners” series.

results.title: The Threepenny opera = L'opera de quat' sous. Die Dreigroschenoper
results.creator_credit: G Pabst, Seymour Nebenzal, Nero-Film, Tobis Filmkunst and Warner Brothers First National Productions
results.brief_description: In 19th Century London, Mack the Knife, a small-time criminal, marries Polly, the daughter of beggar-king, Peachum, and finds himself in a lot of trouble. In the early years of the sound era, film producers often made films in different language versions, using the same sets but different casts for each version. Pabst followed this practice with his twin versions of ‘The Threepenny Opera’ in German and French- ‘Die Driegroschenoper’(98 min.) and ‘L’Opera de Quat’ Sous’(105 min.). The two versions offer rich material for comparison. The German version was originally released in 1931 amidst political and legal controversy. The Nazis, rankled by its socialist message and attacks on government authority, ordered the destruction of the original negative and every print that could be found. The film’s unavailability bolstered its legendary status and, after many years of searching, a complete negative was found and a restored version released in 1960. Music by Kurt Weill. From the play by Bertolt Brecht and Kurt Weill. In French and German with English subtitles.
